name: The Replication Crisis
tagline: Satya + Sword of F&F + Aggravated Assault -> infinite combats
idea: Satya's "whenever attacks" trigger creates a free token copy of any nontoken creature you control, plus 2 free energy.

briefKills.line: Sword of Feast and Famine on Satya + Aggravated Assault → combat untaps all lands → infinite combats → infinite tokens + ETBs
Brudiclad + a token pile + a fat Satya copy (Inferno Titan) → convert every token into the bomb → lethal alpha (no infinite needed)

openings: Weakest axis — Kill reliability (4/5): the judges' soft spot.
Commander-gated; trigger on ATTACK (haste) — protect to swing.
Decap never-closes in 1% of goldfish games — a grind risk.
Tables (all three) never-close 20% of the time.

clock.src: lab rc_speed_lab @8k (ALL-IN ceiling; SQUAD defended proxy ~1 turn slower)
clock.caption: Decap (one opponent) lands around T7; the full table around T10.

gamePlan: Satya's "whenever attacks" trigger creates a free token copy of any nontoken creature you control, plus 2 free energy. The deck stacks ETB creatures for Satya to repeatedly copy, with token doublers and a real combat-loop kill line.
winLine: Satya + Sword of F&F + Aggravated Assault -> infinite combats

finishers.name: Satya + Lightning Runner (infinite combats, 2 cards = commander + 1) — PRIMARY
finishers.note: Both real Satya and real Lightning Runner attack.

finishers.name: Sword of F&F + Aggravated Assault (infinite combats, 3 cards including commander)
finishers.note: Equip Sword of Feast and Famine to Satya (menace + haste makes her hard to chump).

finishers.name: Brudiclad Token Conversion
finishers.note: Sequencing: Turn N combat — Brudiclad's beginning-of-combat trigger fires *before* Satya attacks.

finishers.name: Adeline + Anointed Procession Token Flood
finishers.note: With Adeline + Anointed Procession on board: each attack generates ~6 1/1 humans (3 per opponent in pod, doubled).

finishers.name: Zealous Conscripts Steal
finishers.note: Satya copies Zealous Conscripts → steal an opponent's best permanent.

finishers.name: Value Grind
finishers.note: Repeated ETB abuse turn after turn.

rulings.name: Tokens entering "tapped and attacking" do NOT trigger "whenever ~ attacks" abilities
rulings.note: the combat they're made — but they CAN attack normally in a later combat (once untapped + declared), and then they trigger. Satya copies of Adeline, Phelia, Bident-bearers, etc. don't fire attack triggers on entry, but their ETB triggers DO. This is the whole engine of the Lightning Runner combo: the token Runner makes no energy the combat Satya creates it, but it untaps with the others and does make energy when it attacks in the next combat. (It's also why the old Combat Celebrant "infinite" was fake — exert is a one-time, can't-untap thing.)

rulings.name: The copy must target a nontoken creature
rulings.note: Brudiclad-converted tokens are not legal Satya targets.

rulings.name: Energy is gained free; tokens are kept by paying their mana value in {E} at end step.
rulings.note: An Inferno Titan token costs 6{E} to keep — three attacks bank enough for one. Unkept tokens are sacrificed.

rulings.name: Brudiclad-converted tokens shed Satya's end-of-turn sacrifice clause
rulings.note: (it's a delayed trigger stuck to the original token) — the cleanest way to keep tokens without paying energy.

rulings.name: The Sword + AA loop relies on Satya herself connecting
rulings.note: not the tokens. Menace + haste + 5 toughness makes it reliable, but two blockers stop menace.

rulings.name: Panharmonicon / Elesh Norn double the ETBs of every Satya copy
rulings.note: Strionic Resonator copies Satya's attack trigger for a second token. Phelia and Restoration Angel are the flicker engines (Phelia only flickers when she attacks).

1 Satya, Aetherflux Genius

keep.mixed: commander-gated; trigger on ATTACK (haste) — protect to swing

mull.strategy: Looking for: lands + ramp + a path to Satya on T4, plus at least one ETB creature to start copying. The primary infinite is Satya + Lightning Runner, so a hand holding Lightning Runner or Imperial Recruiter (which fetches it) is a stronger keep than the Sword of Feast and Famine + Aggravated Assault pair (~1–3% assembly, no tutors). Ranger-Captain of Eos fetches nothing in the combo package — don't keep it as a "tutor."

killTree.title: The Replication Crisis — Satya, Aetherflux Genius
killTree.root:
Satya attacking — each attack makes a free token COPY of a nontoken
creature you control + {E}{E} · EVERY line needs Satya to ATTACK (fires on attack, not on connect)
killTree.stall:
Satya must survive as a 3/5 through combat — protect her
(Greaves / Boots / Slip Out the Back) and hold for a safe swing
killTree.caption: cheapest line first · fall to the next

lines.need:
Sword of Feast and Famine on Satya
+ Aggravated Assault
lines.kill: combat untaps all lands → infinite combats → infinite tokens + ETBs

lines.need:
Brudiclad + a token pile
+ a fat Satya copy (Inferno Titan)
lines.kill: convert every token into the bomb → lethal alpha (no infinite needed)
~6 humans per attack + doubled Satya token → wide alpha swing

lines.need:
Satya copies Zealous Conscripts
(+ Strionic Resonator / Aggravated Assault)
lines.kill: steal the pod's best permanents, one per combat

lines.need:
repeated ETB copies (Inferno Titan ping,
Cloudblazer draw, Skyclave exile) (fallback)
lines.kill: grind the pod out over 3–4 combats
